supported_claims: OpenCodeReview leverages large language models to generate review comments, reducing manual review overhead (source: project README).
It supports custom rule sets written in YAML, allowing teams to enforce project-specific coding standards (source: docs/rules.md).
The tool can be deployed as a GitHub Action or GitLab CI job, enabling seamless integration into existing CI/CD pipelines (source: example workflows in repo).
common_pitfalls: Over-reliance on LLM suggestions without human verification can introduce subtle bugs or style inconsistencies.
Misconfiguring rule severity levels may cause too many false positives, leading to review fatigue.
Failing to update the rule set as the project evolves can result in outdated or irrelevant checks.
research_questions: How does OpenCodeReview compare to other AI code review tools like CodeRabbit or Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer?
What is the accuracy of its LLM-generated comments on large, multi-language codebases?
Can the rule engine be extended to support custom static analysis beyond the built-in patterns?
success_criteria: Reduction in time spent on routine code review tasks by at least 30%.
Increase in detection of style violations and common anti-patterns before human review.
Positive developer feedback on the relevance and actionability of automated comments.
